<<THE RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN THE CONSTANTS A, B, AND C IS THAT THE DERIVATIVE OF THE VELOCITY FUNCTION AT T = 3 SECONDS EQUALS ZERO, BECAUSE THE MAXIMUM VELOCITY IS REACHED AT THAT POINT. THIS MEANS THAT THE FIRST DERIVATIVE OF V(T) AT T = 3 IS ZERO. THIS IS A CRUCIAL CONDITION FOR THE MAXIMUM POINT. THE DERIVATIVE OF V(T) IS 3AT^2 - 2BT + CT + D, AND AT T = 3, THIS EQUALS ZERO. SO, 3A(3)^2 - 2B(3) + C(3) + D = 0. THIS CAN BE REWRITTEN AS 27A - 6B + 3C + D = 0. THIS IS THE RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN A, B, C, AND D.>>

<<THE AVERAGE SPEED FROM T = 0 TO T = 5 SECONDS IS THE TOTAL DISTANCE COVERED DIVIDED BY 5 SECONDS. THE TOTAL DISTANCE IS THE INTEGRAL OF V(T) FROM 0 TO 5. THIS IS EQUAL TO THE INTEGRAL OF (AT^3 - BT^2 + CT + D) FROM 0 TO 5. THIS INTEGRAL IS (A/4)(5^4) - (B/3)(5^3) + (C/2)(5^2) + D(5). THIS IS THE TOTAL DISTANCE. DIVIDING THIS BY 5 GIVES THE AVERAGE SPEED. THIS IS A COMPLEX CALCULATION, BUT IT CAN BE EXPRESSED IN TERMS OF A, B, C, AND D. THE AVERAGE SPEED IS (A/4)(625) - (B/3)(125) + (C/2)(25) + D(5) ALL DIVIDED BY 5. THIS IS THE AVERAGE SPEED.>>

<<THE LETTER W APPEARS FOUR TIMES IN THE RESPONSE ABOVE.
